question_text: A racing cyclist uses three methods to reduce friction and air resistance: wearing a streamlined helmet, using smooth tyres, and adopting a crouched riding position. Which of these primarily reduces friction between components, and which reduces air resistance?
correct_answer: Smooth tyres reduce friction between the tyre and road; the streamlined helmet and crouched position reduce air resistance. (Accept: the helmet and body position are streamlined to reduce air resistance; smooth tyres reduce surface friction.)
explanation: Friction acts between surfaces in contact (tyres and road), while air resistance is a type of friction from moving through a fluid. Streamlining reduces air resistance by reducing the surface area pushing against the air.
